title: athroandra molleri
intro: Athroandra molleri, commonly known as the 'New Caledonian tree', is a species of plant in the family Staphylocarpaceae. It is native to New Caledonia, an island region in the Pacific Ocean, and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular choice for treating wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Athroandra molleri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions and health benefits.
side_effects: Athroandra molleri can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea and dizziness due to its potential to interact with blood thinners and medications for high blood pressure. In some cases, prolonged use may lead to gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain. Excessive consumption of the plant can also cause allergic reactions, manifesting as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Furthermore, improper use of Athroandra molleri may exacerbate existing conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, due to its potential toxicity.
